meta.title: GPT Image 2 API | Image Generation, Editing, Text Rendering & UI Mockups | APIDot
meta.description: Generate and edit images with GPT Image 2 on APIDot. It works especially well for readable text inside images, photoreal product visuals, UI mockups, and controlled single-image edits in one async API workflow.

hero.title: GPT Image 2 API for Image Generation and Editing
hero.description: Use GPT Image 2 when you need readable text, photoreal product visuals, cleaner UI mockups, and more controlled image edits in the same workflow. `gpt-image-2` handles new image generation, `gpt-image-2-edit` handles guided edits, and both use the same APIDot async job flow.

overview.title: Use GPT Image 2 when the image needs to read clearly, look believable, and feel usable right away
paragraphs: Teams usually reach for GPT Image 2 when a generic image model is not enough. It is a better fit when the image needs readable text, believable product or lifestyle detail, or a screenshot-style structure that looks closer to a finished asset than a loose concept draft.

On APIDot, `gpt-image-2` is the model for generating new images from a prompt, while `gpt-image-2-edit` is the model for requests that include reference image URLs and need guided changes. That makes the page useful for teams that need both fresh generation and controlled editing without splitting workflows.

The real advantage is business-ready output. GPT Image 2 is strongest when you need cleaner on-image typography, more natural product lighting and materials, interface visuals that feel more like real screenshots, and edits that preserve the original structure instead of rebuilding the entire scene.

capabilities.title: What GPT Image 2 is most useful for on APIDot

items.title: Readable text and labels inside images
items.description: This is one of the clearest reasons teams search for GPT Image 2 in the first place. On APIDot, it is a practical fit for ads, menus, packaging, diagrams, social graphics, and interface concepts where headlines, labels, buttons, or UI copy need to stay readable.

items.title: Photoreal light, material, and color control
items.description: This matters when the image needs to look commercially usable rather than obviously synthetic. On APIDot, that makes GPT Image 2 a stronger fit for product heroes, ecommerce visuals, and realistic campaign imagery.

items.title: UI mockups and screenshot-style composition
items.description: This is valuable when layout logic matters as much as raw style. On APIDot, GPT Image 2 works well for dashboard scenes, landing-page visuals, and interface concepts that need a more convincing screenshot-like structure.

items.title: Product visuals and ecommerce image quality
items.description: Teams also use GPT Image 2 when catalog visuals and merchandising assets need to feel cleaner and more sellable. On APIDot, it is a better fit for product hero shots and commerce-oriented image generation than a looser concept model.

items.title: Single-image edits that keep the original structure
items.description: This matters when the goal is not to start over from scratch. On APIDot, `gpt-image-2-edit` is the mode to use when you want to change the background, context, styling, or local details of an existing image while staying closer to the source composition.

items.title: One async workflow for generation and edit mode
items.description: This is useful for teams that want one integration pattern instead of separate generation and editing systems. On APIDot, both variants use the same async job flow, so it is easy to move from prompt-based generation to guided editing without changing backend wiring.

useCases.title: Common GPT Image 2 API use cases
useCases.description: GPT Image 2 is most useful when the job depends on readable text, believable product or interface visuals, and a clean handoff between generation and editing in the same async workflow.
items: UI mockups and product interface concepts
Ecommerce product images and hero shots

quickstart.title: Start using {modelName} on APIDot in three steps

steps.title: Create your API key
steps.description: Generate an APIDot API key and keep it on the server side. The same key works across image, video, audio, and chat models, so your auth setup stays simple.

steps.title: Top up credits
steps.description: Both `gpt-image-2` and `gpt-image-2-edit` are priced at 2 credits per generation, giving you a flat-cost image workflow that is easy to estimate before rollout.

steps.title: Submit async image jobs
steps.description: Send a prompt, choose `auto` or one of the supported aspect-ratio sizes, and include `image_urls` only for `gpt-image-2-edit`. Then poll task status or receive the completed result through your webhook.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions about GPT Image 2 API

items.question: What is the difference between `gpt-image-2` and `gpt-image-2-edit`?
items.answer: `gpt-image-2` is the text-to-image variant for generating new visuals from a prompt. `gpt-image-2-edit` is the editing variant for requests that include one or more reference image URLs and need controlled changes to an existing image.

items.question: Why do teams choose GPT Image 2 for UI, product, and marketing visuals?
items.answer: Because those workflows usually depend on readable in-image text, believable materials and lighting, and more stable layout structure. GPT Image 2 is a better fit when the image needs to work like a mockup, ad, product visual, or screenshot-style asset, not just look visually interesting.

items.question: What size options are supported on this page?
items.answer: The current tool supports `auto`, `1:1`, `2:3`, `3:2`, `3:4`, `4:3`, `4:5`, `5:4`, `9:16`, and `16:9`, which matches the live GPT Image 2 experience on APIDot.

items.question: Does `gpt-image-2-edit` require reference images, and when should I use edit mode?
items.answer: Yes. Edit mode is for guided changes to an existing image, so the request needs one or more `image_urls`. Use it when you want to preserve the original composition more closely while changing the background, context, styling, or local details. If you want to generate from text alone, stay with `gpt-image-2`.

items.question: Does GPT Image 2 support multiple outputs or `input.n`?
items.answer: No. The current APIDot GPT Image 2 flow returns one image per request, and `input.n` is not supported in this model shape.

items.question: How much does GPT Image 2 cost on APIDot?
items.answer: Both variants are currently priced at 2 credits per generation on APIDot, which maps to $0.01 with the platform's standard credit conversion.

items.question: When are credits deducted?
items.answer: Credits are deducted for successful generations. You can confirm the final task state through polling or webhook delivery before consuming the resulting image URL in your application.

items.question: How do I integrate GPT Image 2 into production?
items.answer: Use APIDot's async workflow: submit the job, store the returned `task_id`, track completion through `/api/generate/status/<task_id>` or a callback URL, and consume the final image URL only after the task reaches a terminal state.
